summary: Jeff Atwood argues that default settings are among the most consequential decisions software developers make. Using Vista's built-in search as an example, he demonstrates how defaults that 'just work' without installation or configuration provide massive value to users. He contends that defaults drive software industry evolution by establishing baselines that competitors must match or exceed. The post criticizes the Unix/Linux model of infinite configurability as lacking meaningful standards. Atwood emphasizes that for most users, the default is the only value they'll ever use, making thoughtful default selection critical to both user experience and business success.
key_points: Third-party tools may offer similar functionality, but built-in defaults reach millions of users who would never install alternatives
Good defaults eliminate installation and configuration friction - they 'just work'
Defaults establish industry baselines that competitors must match, driving software evolution forward
Infinite configurability (like Unix/Linux) creates a problem by eliminating meaningful standards for comparison
Companies like Google, Microsoft, and Yahoo pay millions to influence defaults because they directly impact the bottom line
money_quotes: There's nothing to install. There's nothing to configure. It just works.
Defaults are arguably the most important design decisions you'll ever make as a software developer.
Defaults are how the software industry evolves.
For most users, the default value is the only value.

Choose good defaults, and users will sing the praises of your software and how easy it is to use. Choose poor defaults, and you'll face down user angst over configuration, and probably a host of tech support calls as well.
You should agonize over every default in your software. If you aren't, you're doing the user, and yourself, a disservice.
key_insight: For most users, the default value is the only value they'll ever encounter, making thoughtful default selection one of the most impactful decisions a software developer can make.
